HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_WELCOME_TEXT_2: The knowledge contained in these pages will get you acquainted with the **game's core elements**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_WELCOME_TEXT_3: To re-open this window at any time, click the **Question Mark** button in the top-left corner of the screen.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_SAVELOAD_TEXT_1: By default, your village is automatically saved every five minutes. You can also manually save your game from the "Save Game" menu or whenever you leave the game from the menu.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_SAVELOAD_TEXT_2: You can load your last village from the "Load Game" menu. You can also access and load an older save from the "See Saved Games" button.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_SETTINGS_TEXT_1: You can change your game settings, such as Language, User Interface Scale, Video Quality, Audio Volume, and Custom Controls.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTTERRITORY_TEXT_: To start building your village, you must choose your first **Territory**. Each available starting Territory has all the **basic resources** you need to begin settling the new land.
Later, you will be able to **expand** further by purchasing new **Territories**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_ZONING_TEXT_1: **Wood** resources require you to **paint** an area that is fit for **woodcutting** using the **Paint tool**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BASICCONTROL_MOUSECONTROL_TEXT_1: You can interact with buttons, buildings, and villagers using the **left mouse button**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BASICCONTROL_MOUSECONTROL_TEXT_2: You can close any window by **right-clicking** on it.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BASICCONTROL_CAMERAMOVEMENT_TEXT_1: You can move the game camera by **moving your mouse to the edge of the screen** or by using keyboard shortcuts defined in the **Settings Menu** (W, A, S and D by default).
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BASICCONTROL_CAMERAMOVEMENT_TEXT_2: You can also move the game camera by pressing CTRL and clicking the middle mouse.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BASICCONTROL_CAMERAZOOM_TEXT_1: You can zoom in and out by **scrolling the middle mouse up and down** or by using keyboard shortcuts defined in the **Settings Menu** (Page Up and Down by default).
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BASICCONTROL_CAMERAANGLE_TEXT_1: You can change the camera angle by **pressing the middle mouse button and moving your mouse**, or by using keyboard shortcuts defined in the **Settings Menu** (Q, U, R and F by default).
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_SETTLINGDOWN_TEXT_1: To start building your village, you must choose your first **Territory**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_SETTLINGDOWN_TEXT_2: Each available starting Territory has all the **basic resources** you need.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_SETTLINGDOWN_TEXT_3: Once you have chosen your starting Territory, you must place your **Village Centre**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_SETTLINGDOWN_TEXT_4: It will act as a **storage point** for your initial resources until more suitable storage options become available.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_BUILDERS_TEXT_1: Buildings are selected from the **Build Menu** in the **Action Module**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_BUILDERS_TEXT_2: You can **move** and **rotate** buildings before starting their construction.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_BUILDERS_TEXT_3: Once their construction begins, **Builders** will **transport** the **necessary resources** from storage to construction sites.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_BUILDERS_TEXT_4: Buildings have their construction resources divided into **Bundles**, affecting the **order** in which resources are transported to construction sites.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_BUILDERS_TEXT_5: Once all resources of a Bundle have been **gathered** at the construction site, a **Builder** will be assigned to **construct** the building.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_ASSIGNINGWORKERS_TEXT_: There are multiple ways of assigning villagers to **Workplaces** or **job roles**.
By **clicking the "+" button** on the Workplace Window. This will quick-assign a villager to a Worker Slot.
By **clicking on a Worker Slot**. This will allow you to assign a specific villager.
By **assigning a specific job** to a specific villager. This villager will be assigned to a corresponding Workplace with an available Worker Slot.
You can **unassign villagers** from jobs and workplaces at any time.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_PAINTTOOL_TEXT_1: The **Paint Tool** is used to designate zones for various purposes, such as woodcutting, or to guide where your villagers can build their homes.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_PAINTTOOL_TEXT_2: You can select a **Round Brush** or **Square Brush** from the Paint Menu.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_PAINTTOOL_TEXT_3: **Remove paint** by right-clicking and dragging over the zone you wish to erase.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_PAINTTOOL_TEXT_4: **Increase** and **Decrease** the brush size by using keyboard shortcuts defined in the **Settings Menu** (CTRL by default).
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_PAINTTOOL_TEXT_5: Some Paint tools are **exclusive to a specific building**, such as the Crop Field to the Wheat Farm and the Reforestation Area to the Forester Camp.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_PAINTTOOL_TEXT_6: Some workplaces, such as the Lumber Camp, depend on **global Painting** areas, which are found in the **Paint Menu** within the **action module**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGEBASIC_PAINTTOOL_TEXT_8: These **building-specific painting areas** can be **accessed** directly from the workplace's **Building Window**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.TOPIC_TIP_ASSIGNING_WORKERS_1: Click the **+ button** in the building panel to automatically **assign the nearest villager** to a **workplace**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_EXTRACTION_TEXT_1: Resources such as **Stone**, **Fish** and **Berries** are collected by **building their corresponding workplace** close to their environmental location.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_EXTRACTION_TEXT_2: Other resources, such as **Wood** and **Boar**, require you to **build a Workplace** nearby and **paint an area** that specifies which resources to collect.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_EXTRACTION_TEXT_3: Most environmental resources depend on their surroundings to exist; **altering the environment** will affect their numbers.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_EXTRACTION_TEXT_4: Make sure the resources you want to collect are located **within your Territories**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_TRANSPORT_TEXT_1: Once a resource is produced, it can be transported to **storage** for further use.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_TRANSPORT_TEXT_2: Storage buildings, such as the **Granary** and the **Warehouse**, can facilitate the movement of resources between different workplaces of a production chain.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_REFINING_TEXT_1: Some Workplaces **transform or refine resources into something else**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_REFINING_TEXT_3: Villagers assigned to these Workplaces will **start by fetching the required resources** for transformation. All necessary resources must be gathered first before **production can commence**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_REFINING_TEXT_4: You can view the **required** and **produced** resources in the workplace's **description**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_DISTRIBUTION_TEXT_1: Some resources have to be stored at specific workplaces in order to be consumed, such as Berries stored at a **Food Stall** in a **Market**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_DISTRIBUTION_TEXT_2: Once stored, the resource will eventually be consumed by villagers looking to fill that Need.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MONUMENTBASIC_CREATINGMONUMENTS_TEX: **Monuments** represent the greatness of your village. They are an **assemblage** of multiple **buildings** called **Sub-Buildings**, each with its own Function.
The first step to making a **Monument** is to choose an initial **Sub-Building** you wish to add, then design it as you would for a **Multi-Part Building**.
Each Sub-Building must meet **construction requirements** to ensure the building can function.
Don't worry about deciding which **Sub-Building** to build first, as you can **add** as many Sub-Buildings as you wish and then **edit** them afterwards.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MONUMENTBASIC_EDITINGMONUMENTS_TEXT: You can edit any constructed buildings by pressing the **Edit Building** button:
You can **move** and **rotate** any Building Parts.
Some Building Parts can be **scaled up and down**.
Multi-Part Buildings can be **improved further with new Parts**.
Monuments can be **improved** with **additional Parts** and **Sub-Buildings**.
Double-clicking a Building Part will let you select all **connected Parts** simultaneously.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MONUMENTBASIC_MONUMENTPREVIEW_TEXT_: **Planned constructions** that have not been started are **visible at all times**.

**Buildings under construction** are only visible in **Edit Building Mode** or by toggling the **Construction Preview** layer in the **Visibility Panel**, located at the top-left corner of the screen.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MONUMENTBASIC_MONUMENTEFFECT_TEXT_1: Most buildings have a **Maintenance Cost** that impacts your **Budget**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MONUMENTBASIC_MONUMENTEFFECT_TEXT_2: Estate-related decorations and certain advanced buildings can also provide **Splendour**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MONUMENTBASIC_MONUMENTEFFECT_TEXT_3: Monument Sub-Buildings also have a specific **Size** based on the Building Parts it contains.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MONUMENTBASIC_MONUMENTEFFECT_TEXT_4: Each building serves a specific **Function**, which often scales based on its **Size**. For example, a large Tax Office can have more villagers assigned than a small one.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CONSTRUCTIONREQ_PARTREQ_TEXT_1: Every building has a **Part Requirement** according to their **Function**. For example:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CONSTRUCTIONREQ_PARTREQ_TEXT_3: The **Great Hall** requires a **Room** and a **Visitor Location**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CONSTRUCTIONREQ_PARTREQ_TEXT_4: The **Rustic Church** requires a **Room** and a **Bell Tower**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CONSTRUCTIONREQ_PARTREQ_TEXT_5: You can find the required Parts by browsing their respective tabs in the **Build Menu**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CONSTRUCTIONREQ_INTERIORACCESS_TEXT: Most Monument Sub-Buildings and Multi-Part Buildings require **Interior Access**.
**Any connected Door** can lead to **any connected Room** of Sub-Buildings from the same Monument.
A **green highlight** on Interior Access means it **connects** to entrances, while **red** means it **does not**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CONSTRUCTIONREQ_FORTIFICATION_TEXT_: Some buildings require **Fortification** coverage as a construction requirement.
This **coverage** is granted differently per building:
Churches and Manor House **towers** provide **small Fortification ranges**.
Defensive **Walls** and **Watchtowers** yield much **larger Fortification ranges**.
Certain Sub-Buildings, such as the **Tax Office**, can **simultaneously require and provide Fortification**.
Some advanced buildings also require **Heavy Fortification**, which is granted by major defensive structures, such as **stone-built Walls** and **stone-built Castle Parts**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_NEEDS_TEXT_1: Every member of your village is looking to satisfy **Essential** and **Additional Needs**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_NEEDS_TEXT_2: Fulfil **Essential Needs** whenever possible. If left unanswered, they will apply **Happiness penalties** and your villagers might **leave**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_NEEDS_TEXT_3: **Additional Needs** can be fulfilled at your own pace, though after being filled at least once, they will apply **Happiness penalties** if left unanswered.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_NEEDS_TEXT_4: Villagers fulfil their Needs by **consuming resources**, such as Berries for the **Rustic Food Need**, or **visiting buildings**, such as churches for the **Service Need**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_NEEDS_TEXT_5: Check for details by **hovering over each Need** in the **Villager Window**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_HAPPINESS_TEXT_1: Each **fulfilled Need** contributes to the total **Happiness** of your villagers.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_HAPPINESS_TEXT_2: The village's **overall Happiness** is shown on the **Villager Module** at the top of the screen. By **hovering** over this module, you can see which Needs are currently **lacking**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_HAPPINESS_TEXT_5: It enables you to collect **higher taxes** from villagers.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_HAPPINESS_TEXT_6: On the other hand, **unhappy** villagers might **leave your village** instead.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_IMMIGRATION_TEXT_1: Your **population** increases through **Immigration**, influenced by two factors:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_IMMIGRATION_TEXT_2: **Happiness**, which is improved by fulfilling villagers' Needs.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_IMMIGRATION_TEXT_3: **Employment**, which is improved by providing jobs to as many villagers as possible.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_IMMIGRATION_TEXT_4: Every **few days**, Newcomers will **immigrate** and request an audience with you. Should you **welcome** them, they join your village as **Serfs**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_IMMIGRATION_TEXT_5: **Newcomers will wait** at your Village Centre for a few days. If you don't let them join, they will eventually **move on**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGAREA_TEXT_1: **Housing** is built automatically by your villagers within **Residential areas** you have **painted** beforehand.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGAREA_TEXT_2: To mark a **Residential area**, click the **Paint Brush** icon on the Action Module, select the **Residential paint brush**, and **paint** the desired land.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGAREA_TEXT_3: Ensure these **zones** are spacious and in close proximity to the residents' **Workplaces** and **Needs**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_SAFETY_TEXT_1: To **improve Housing Density**, Villagers need to have their houses within **Fortification range** and **Patrol coverage**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_SAFETY_TEXT_3: **Fortification** is given to buildings located **within Fortification ranges** of **towers**, **watchtowers**, and **walls**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_SAFETY_TEXT_4: **Patrol Coverage** is earned by assigning villagers to Watchposts as **Patrollers**, and painting **Patrolling areas** onto houses within Fortification range.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_SAFETY_TEXT_5: **Combatants** also perform patrols when their Companies are assigned to **Patrol Duty**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_SAFETY_TEXT_6: The more **Patrollers** in a Patrol, the **greater** their **Patrol Efficiency**; better **Patrol Efficiency** means larger and stronger **coverage** per patrol.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_WEALTH_INCOMES_TEXT_6: **Trading** surplus can generate revenue **throughout the game**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_WEALTH_INCOMES_TEXT_8: The **Levy Mandate** provides a **quick way** to gather **funds**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_WEALTH_COSTS_TEXT_1: Coins can be spent on different useful elements:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_WEALTH_UPKEEPS_TEXT_1: Workplaces, Monuments, and Territories have **Maintenance Costs** in **Coins**. These increase as your village progresses:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_WEALTH_UPKEEPS_TEXT_3: **Monuments** are more expensive the greater **Size** and **Splendour** they provide.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_WEALTH_UPKEEPS_TEXT_4: The more **Territories** you acquire, the greater the **Upkeep** each **Territory** has.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_WEALTH_TREASURY_TEXT_1: Your village can only **hold** a **limited** amount of **Coins** at a time.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_WEALTH_TREASURY_TEXT_2: You can **increase** your maximum **limit** of **Coins** by constructing **Treasury Buildings** in certain **Monuments**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_WEALTH_TREASURY_TEXT_3: The **Treasury Capacity** depends on the **Size** of its **Sub-Building**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BOOKBASIC_OVERVIEW_TEXT_1: The **Overview Page** contains general information about your village. It allows you to:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BOOKBASIC_OVERVIEW_TEXT_2: View and edit your **Title**, **Name**, and **Village Name**
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BOOKBASIC_MANAGEMENT_TEXT_1: From the **Resources**, **Villagers**, and **Buildings** pages of the **Royal Book**, you can access lists that give you a better overview of your village:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BOOKBASIC_MANAGEMENT_TEXT_2: **The Resources Page** lets you check how many of each resource you have and allows you to **Favourite**, **Track**, and **Stockpile** them.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BOOKBASIC_MANAGEMENT_TEXT_3: **The Villagers Page** lets you **sort your population** by Status, Name, Job, Happiness, and current issues. It also helps you **track** their position, home, and work.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BOOKBASIC_MANAGEMENT_TEXT_4: **The Buildings Page** lets you **sort your Workplaces** by Name, Workers, and Status. It also allows you to **manage workers and production**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BOOKBASIC_BUDGET_TEXT_1: You can review your **monthly income** and **expenses** on the **Budget Page**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BOOKBASIC_BUDGET_TEXT_2: **Wages** and **Upkeep** Coins **progressively accumulate** as the days go by.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BOOKBASIC_BUDGET_TEXT_3: **Rewards**, **sales**, and **purchases** are added all at once.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BOOKBASIC_BUDGET_TEXT_4: Make sure to visit the **Budget Page** often, as it only displays the **current month** and the **one beforehand**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BOOKBASIC_LOGS_TEXT_1: The **Logs Page** allows you to check **everything** that has happened to your village since the **beginning**:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_BOOKBASIC_LOGS_TEXT_7: Purchases and Sales via Trade Routes
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_TRADING_TRADEROUTES_TEXT_1: **Trade Routes** are unlocked in the Royal Book by **spending resources**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_TRADING_TRADEROUTES_TEXT_2: Once unlocked, a trader will visit your village **every few weeks** and trade the resources you have listed for **purchase** and **sale**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_TRADING_BUYINGSELLING_TEXT_1: Once a **Trade Route** with a **Neighbouring Village** has been unlocked, you can **buy** or **sell** the specific resources they are supplying or looking for.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_TRADING_BUYINGSELLING_TEXT_2: After defining a resource you want to buy or sell, you must specify the **quantity** of resources you wish to **keep in stock**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_TRADING_BUYINGSELLING_TEXT_3: Ensure that the traded resources are **assigned** to **Storage buildings**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_TRADING_BUYINGSELLING_TEXT_4: **Traders** will then **visit** your village **regularly** to seek out these storages.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_TRADING_TRADEROUTEUPGRADE_TEXT_1: The **volume** a trader can buy or sell is **limited** per resource, per visit.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_TRADING_TRADEROUTEUPGRADE_TEXT_2: Once enough resources have been traded over time, you will gain access to a **Mandate** to **upgrade the Trade Route**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_TRADING_TRADEROUTEUPGRADE_TEXT_3: **Completing** this Mandate will **increase** the **volume** of resources **bought** and **sold** through that Trade Route.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_YOURMANDATES_TEXT_1: Building a **Great Hall** gives you access to **Mandates**, which are tasks that improve your village.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_YOURMANDATES_TEXT_2: You can perform the following Mandates by yourself:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_YOURMANDATES_TEXT_3: **Levy Coins** from your villagers at the cost of a temporary **Happiness penalty**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_YOURMANDATES_TEXT_4: **Promote** your **Villagers**, **Combatants**, and **Monastics** to a **higher status** – at a certain cost.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_RIGHTHAND_TEXT_1: Each **Estate** lets you **nominate** a member of your village to perform special **Mandates**:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_RIGHTHAND_TEXT_2: A **Villager** can be nominated to the **Labour** position of **Bailiff**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_RIGHTHAND_TEXT_3: A **Monastic** can be nominated to the **Clergy** position of **Monastic Emissary**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_RIGHTHAND_TEXT_4: A **Villager** can be nominated to the **Kingdom** position of **Royal Courtier**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_RIGHTHANDMANDATES_TEXT_1: You can perform additional **Estate-related Mandates** by nominating a member of your village:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_RIGHTHANDMANDATES_TEXT_2: **Prospect Mineral** lets you discover resources contained within nearby **Mineral Deposits**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_RIGHTHANDMANDATES_TEXT_3: **Revise an Edict/Privilege** lets you change one enacted **Edict/Privilege**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_RIGHTHANDMANDATES_TEXT_4: **Influence an Estate** lets you earn **Influence** in exchange for **Coins**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_RIGHTHANDMANDATES_TEXT_5: **Welcome Commoners/Militiamen/Novices** lets new **Villagers**, **Monastics**, or **Combatants** immigrate in exchange for **Influence**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_RIGHTHANDMANDATES_TEXT_6: **Expel Unemployed Monastics** causes unemployed **Monastics** to **leave** the village.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_PROSPECTION_TEXT_1: Each **Map** has several **Mineral Deposits** containing **unknown resources**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_PROSPECTION_TEXT_2: Prospecting them with the relevant Mandate **reveals the resources** they contain.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_PROSPECTION_TEXT_3: This Estate Mandate can only be performed by the **Bailiff** and **Royal Courtier**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_PROSPECTION_TEXT_4: **Mineral Deposits** must be **reachable** for their Mandate to be available. You might need to build a bridge across a river or over a cliff.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MANDATES_PROSPECTION_TEXT_5: Once the Mineral Deposit is **prospected**, click on it and build a **Quarry** on top to collect the resource.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_PROGRESSION_PROGRESSIONMENU_TEXT_1: Some **buildings** are not available at the start of the game. You will need to **unlock** them from the **Progression Screen**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_PROGRESSION_PROGRESSIONMENU_TEXT_2: Unlocking them requires you to reach their **Tier**, which might require a certain number of **Prosperity** and **Estate Splendour**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_PROGRESSION_PROGRESSIONMENU_TEXT_3: Once there, you must pay a certain amount of **Coins** or **Influence** to unlock them for your village.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_PROGRESSION_PROSPERITY_TEXT_1: **Prosperity** is the overall score of your village and your primary benchmark of **progress**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_PROGRESSION_PROSPERITY_TEXT_7: **Splendour** is your total amount of **Splendour** per **Estate**
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_PROGRESSION_SPLENDOR_TEXT_1: **Splendour** is a value gained by building **Decorations** and impressive Estate **Monuments**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_PROGRESSION_SPLENDOR_TEXT_2: The **Splendour** of the following **Estates** can be **increased** as such:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_PROGRESSION_SPLENDOR_TEXT_6: **Splendour** is given in greater amounts the more **impressive** a building **Part** or **Decoration** is.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_PROGRESSION_INFLUENCE_TEXT_1: **Influence**—earned by completing **Mandates** and serving the **Estates** through **Quests**—represents your overall standing with the **Estates of the Realm**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_PROGRESSION_INFLUENCE_TEXT_2: Influence has a **soft cap**, determined by your **Splendour** with the same **Estate**, and slowly approaches that **cap** over time.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_PROGRESSION_INFLUENCE_TEXT_3: You can use an **Estate's Influence** for different purposes:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_PROGRESSION_INFLUENCE_TEXT_5: To **petition Estates** for benefits in **Mandates** and **Quests**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_TAXATION_TEXT_1: **Taxation** is a type of **income** available for the **Labour Estate**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_TAXATION_TEXT_2: You can dispatch **Tax Collectors** from the **Tax Office** to **collect Coins** from your villagers.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_TAXATION_TEXT_3: The **weight** of each **Residential Tax** can be set on the **Taxes Page** of the **Royal Book**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_TAXATION_TEXT_4: **Increasing Taxes** will let you earn more **Coins** at the cost of villagers' **Happiness**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_TAXATION_TEXT_5: **Higher-status** villagers mean **higher revenue**, while **higher-density housing** means more **Coins cumulating** in one place.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCEFLOW_TRACKINGRESOURCE_TEXT_: The **Resource Tracker** gives you a **real-time view** of where resources are located within your village.
You can **view** where **resources** are stored in your village by either:
**Right-clicking** on a **resource** in the **Resource Module** on the top of your screen.
Pressing the Track button by each resource on the Resource Page.
This can help you to understand several things:
Why certain **Villager Needs** are not being met.
How long until **Delivery Quests** can be fulfilled.
Make sure the **Resource Tracker** is **activated** in the **Visibility** panel.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCEFLOW_STORAGES_TEXT_1: **Storage buildings**, such as the **Granary** and **Warehouse**, let you accumulate large amounts of resources in one place.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCEFLOW_STORAGES_TEXT_2: **Resource storage** is a **mandatory step** in **trading**, as Traders will only visit these locations.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCEFLOW_STORAGES_TEXT_3: On top of this, Storage buildings can also **improve the resource flow** of a village by **reducing transportation time** between Workplaces.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCEFLOW_STOCKPILING_TEXT_1: **Stockpiling** will **prevent resources** from being **transported**, which can be useful when trying to complete Quests.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCEFLOW_STOCKPILING_TEXT_2: You can **Stockpile** resources directly from the **Royal Book**. Under the **Resources Page**, click the **Stockpile** button at the left of any given resource.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCEFLOW_STOCKPILING_TEXT_3: Don't forget to **disable Stockpiling** afterwards, as villagers are **forbidden** from using stockpiled resources.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCEFLOW_STORAGEFUNCTIONS_TEXT_: Use the **Priority buttons** associated with different **Storage buildings** to use resources most efficiently.
**Accept** allows the hauling and stocking of resources from other **Workplaces**.
**Refuse** prevents resources from being further stocked.
**Stock Maximum** causes resources to be fetched from **Workplaces** and other **Storage buildings**.
**Empty Stock** prevents resources from being stocked further, whilst other **Transporters** are allowed to **haul** them away.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_ENVOYS_TEXT_1: Your **village** is part of a lively realm from the **Middle Ages** that seeks to cohabit with you.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_ENVOYS_TEXT_2: Occasionally, **Envoys** will visit your village and offer you **Quests**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_ENVOYS_TEXT_3: In addition to **resource** rewards, Quests often provide **Influence**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_ENVOYS_TEXT_4: If you are **too busy** to answer a Quest immediately, it will **remain available** for some time.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_CHOICES_TEXT_2: You can learn more about **choices** and see what they imply by **hovering** your mouse over them. Think twice before making a **promise**!
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_CHOICES_TEXT_3: If you miss or turn down a Quest, it will **return eventually**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_DELIVERY_TEXT_1: One of the most common types of Quests is **Delivery**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_DELIVERY_TEXT_2: During deliveries, you have a **limited time** to **gather** and **produce** the requested resources in your **Storage buildings**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_DELIVERY_TEXT_3: Once you have stored the requested resources, you can **complete the delivery** by clicking the **Confirm** button on the Quest Panel.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ADVANCEDCONSTRUCTION_UNDOREDO_TEXT_: While **planning** or **editing** a building, you can always **revert unbuilt modifications** by pressing the **Undo** button.
Likewise, you can **redo reverted modifications** by pressing the **Redo** button.
Modifications are **applied for good** once construction begins.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ADVANCEDCONSTRUCTION_CONSTRUCTIONPR: **Builders** balance their work time between three types of construction:  **Monuments**, **Housing**, and **Workplaces**.

Builders begin with the **first** constructions you started, **per type**. This allows you to undertake **multiple construction projects** simultaneously **without monopolizing** all **Builders** on a single task.
They will **skip** a construction when they **lack the resources** to complete it, but they will return to it once the resource becomes available again.
You can alter this queue by using the **Prioritize Construction** and **Pause Construction** buttons located on the **Building Window** of each construction site.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ADVANCEDCONSTRUCTION_PARTSNAPPING_T: While **planning** or **editing**, you can activate **Toggle Building Part Snap** to have parts automatically connect when placed near **Snap Points**.
**Toggling** the function **off** will let you place Parts **wherever you want**.
However, **certain Parts**, such as dormers and roof chimneys, can only be placed while **snapped**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ADVANCEDCONSTRUCTION_PARTSCALING_TE: You can **scale up** many building Parts, giving them **additional floors**.
Doing so will increase their **Size**, but will also increase their **Construction Cost** and **Maintenance Cost**.
By using the **Part Levelling Mode**, you can adjust multiple Parts to **fit the height** of a specific reference Part. This is ideal when building on slopes and uneven terrain.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_USERCOMFORT_UISCALING_TEXT_1: If you have trouble reading the UI or it appears too big, you can modify its size by navigating to **Main Menu > Settings > Video > User Interface Scale**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_USERCOMFORT_AUDIO_TEXT_1: Do you wish to pump up the music or find the audible notifications tough to hear?
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_USERCOMFORT_AUDIO_TEXT_2: You can adjust various game audio levels using **Main Menu > Settings > Audio**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_USERCOMFORT_MOTIONSICKNESS_TEXT_1: Do you feel like you're getting motion sickness while looking at the game?
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_USERCOMFORT_MOTIONSICKNESS_TEXT_2: Try disabling tree sway in **Main Menu > Settings > Video > Environmental Movement**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_QUEST_MODULE_TEXT_1: Your **objectives** are displayed on the **Quest Module**!
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_QUEST_MODULE_TEXT_2: The **Quest Module** will display your current **Objectives** and **Quests** throughout the game.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_ACTION_MODULE_TEXT_1: Most actions, like **placing new buildings** or **expanding your Territory**, can be performed by the **Action Module** at the bottom centre of the screen.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_ACTION_MODULE_TEXT_2: Note that not all actions may be available at first.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_MULTIPLE_PART_BUILDI: Some buildings, such as the **Market**, have **multiple Parts** and can be customized that way.
Each Part has a cost and can have a **specific effect**.
Check for details on the **Part description**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_WORLD_GUI_TEXT_1: A **villager** wants to speak with you! Click on the **bubble** to start the interaction.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_BOOK_TEXT_1: You now have access to the **Royal Book**!
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_BOOK_TEXT_2: The **Royal Book** contains useful information and various features, like the capability to establish **Trade Routes** with your neighbours.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_REFER_HELP_TEXT_1: Refer to the **Help Panel** at any time for **advanced topics** and **advice**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_ZONING_PER_BUILDING_: Some **buildings** require a **designated zone** to operate, which you can organise via their **Building Window**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_TIME_CONTROL_TEXT1: You can **pause** the game or adjust its **speed** at any time via the **Game Speed Module**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_HOUSE_ZONING_TEXT_1: Villagers will automatically establish their home in the **residential area** you have defined. They will also pick a location that best suits their interest, like the **proximity to their workplace**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MONUMENTBASIC_IMPROVINGMONUMENTS_TE: Like normal buildings, monuments can be **improved** by **adding** new **sub-buildings** and **editing** existing ones.
You can also **change** the function of a sub-building or **destroy** any sub-building you want.
Whenever you **destroy Building Parts**, you are **refunded half** of their **construction costs**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ADVANCEDCONSTRUCTION_REPLACEMODE_TE: Épületek tervezése vagy szerkesztése közben **cserélhetsz részeket** más, **ugyanabba a kategóriába** tartozóakkal.
Amíg megtartod a kijelölést, kipróbálhatsz különböző részeket és **vizualizálhatod** a **különbségeket**.
Ha az új rész megengedi, a korábban csatlakoztatott **ajtók** és **díszítések** **csatlakozva maradnak**.

A **cseredarabok** **költsége** **csökkentve van** a **kicserélt részekből** visszanyert építőanyagokkal. Csak a **különbözetet** **fizeted ki** és **visszatérítést** kapsz a **felbontott anyagok** **feléért**.
A **részcsere eszközt** használhatod az **erődítések** **falainak** megváltoztatására anélkül, hogy elveszítenéd az **erődítési lefedettséget**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ADVANCEDCONSTRUCTION_MONUMENTSELECT: **Kijelölhetsz** egy teljes **főépületet**, annak **alépületeit**, vagy annak **részeit**, és **mozgathatod** őket tetszésed szerint.

Egyszerre **több egyedi részt** is **kijelölhetsz** a **beállítási menüben** meghatározott billentyű **lenyomva tartásával** (alapértelmezetten CTRL) és a részekre **kattintással**. Ez a **részek** kijelölése több **alépületből** állhat ugyanabból a főépületből.
Egy **teljes főépületet** vagy **több alépületet** is kijelölhetsz egyszerre az alépületek listája melletti **jelölőnégyzetek** használatával.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_INFORMATIONLAYERS_TEXT_1: A falvad számos aspektusát figyelheted a **Láthatóság** panel különböző opcióival.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_INFORMATIONLAYERS_TEXT_2: A **Világ rétegek** megmutatja a **Szépítés**, **Nemkívánatos környék**, és **Erődítés** lefedettségi területeit.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_INFORMATIONLAYERS_TEXT_3: A **Kapcsolható** megmutatja és elrejti a kiegészítő információkat, mint például a **Területhatárok**, **Lakhatási elfoglaltság**, és **Építési előnézet**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_INFORMATIONLAYERS_TEXT_4: Az **Épület állapot** megmutatja és elrejti az épület állapotokat, mint például az **Építés alatt álló részek**, **Szüneteltetett termelések**, és **Munkások nélküli munkahelyek**.
